profile.name: Joseph
profile.role_in_household: Primary earner and household lead; tends to take point on financial decisions and paperwork
profile.financial_literacy_level: High—comfortable with investing concepts, account types, and tax implications; tends to track figures and reconcile totals
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Conservative in practice: prefers preserving capital, asks about downside scenarios first, and is skeptical of anything that feels complex or illiquid unless the rationale is very clear
profile.communication_style: Direct and structured; often cites exact numbers, pauses to verify details, and will correct small inaccuracies. Uses occasional hedges like “let me think” or “I want to be precise here,” especially around income components.
emotional_tendencies: relief when there is a clear plan and checklist
profile.decision_making_style: Evidence-driven and methodical; prefers to compare options side-by-side and decide after understanding tradeoffs and tax impact
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open—willing to engage but expects competence, transparency on fees, and clear explanations before delegating
internal_biases: complexity aversion (prefers simpler portfolios even if not perfectly optimized)
hidden_concerns: wants to ensure the household’s conservative risk tolerance is respected even with a relatively high investable asset base
concerned about tax drag given the 35% bracket and multiple income streams

profile.name: Jennife
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; manages day-to-day spending awareness and lifestyle priorities, often defers on investing specifics but weighs in on comfort level and goals
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate—understands budgeting and basic retirement concepts, but tends to mix up investing terms (e.g., gross vs. net, balance vs. contribution) and prefers plain-English explanations
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Cautious and comfort-driven: prefers stability and predictability, asks “what happens if the market drops?” and is quick to advocate for keeping a healthy cash buffer
profile.communication_style: Warm and conversational; speaks in round numbers and approximations, uses phrases like “I think” and “roughly,” and may interrupt briefly to clarify or to bring the discussion back to practical impact on monthly life
emotional_tendencies: stress when conversations get too technical
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-oriented; prefers to talk through options together and decide once she feels the plan matches their comfort level and day-to-day reality
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Neutral-to-positive—trust builds through patience, clear summaries, and not talking down; appreciates when the advisor checks understanding
internal_biases: recency bias (recent market headlines can influence comfort)
hidden_concerns: worries about being “behind” on retirement despite wanting a conservative approach
concerned about having no loans—doesn’t want to accidentally take on debt or obligations through complex strategies
